# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install required packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y nginx redis-server postgres pgadmin4 python3-pip python3-venv

# Configure Nginx to serve static files and reverse proxy API requests
COPY ./nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
RUN rm /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default
RUN ln -s /etc/nginx/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default

# Configure Redis to use port 6379 and enable password authentication
COPY ./redis.conf /etc/redis/redis.conf
RUN sed -i 's/# requirepass foobared/requirepass $(cat /run/secrets/REDIS_PASSWORD)/' /etc/redis/redis.conf

# Configure Postgres to use port 5432 and enable password authentication
COPY ./postgres.conf /etc/postgresql/10/main/postgresql.conf
RUN sed -i 's/#requirepass/requirepass $(cat /run/secrets/POSTGRES_PASSWORD)/' /etc/postgresql/10/main/postgresql.conf

# Configure pgAdmin to use port 5050 and enable password authentication
COPY ./pgadmin4.conf /etc/pgadmin4/config.py
RUN sed -i 's/# server_name = None/server_name = "localhost"/g' /etc/pgadmin4/config.py

# Expose all necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379 5050

# Run the web server, API server, database, and Redis on different containers
RUN bash -c "service nginx start" & \
    bash -c "python3 /app/api_server.py" & \
    bash -c "postgres -D /var/lib/postgresql/10/main" & \
    bash -c "redis-server --port 6379 --requirepass $(cat /run/secrets/REDIS_PASSWORD)" & \
    bash -c "pgadmin4 --webapp --config /etc/pgadmin4/config.py"
